Archaic Ammunition Types.Description: Basic projectiles used in slings and other early weapons. Limited in penetration but useful for hunting and defense.
Lead projectiles used in early firearms, offering basic penetration but inferior compared to modern ammunition.
Iron projectiles designed for higher penetration, especially against soft targets.
Lead shot hardened to improve penetration, but still significantly weaker than modern alternatives.
Early arrowheads made from bronze, capable of inflicting damage but limited in penetrating armor.
Iron arrowheads, more durable and effective against armor, used with bows and crossbows in medieval times.
Round iron balls used in early cannons, designed for siege warfare and capable of damaging structures and infantry.
Primitive ammunition used with slings, typically stone or clay, with irregular trajectories.
Gunpowder-based rounds used in early firearms, providing more penetration than earlier ammunition but still lacking power compared to modern rounds.
